mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower cost of capital directly increases present value of corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo suggests potential for further easing in crisis environment
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines do not exceed baseline returns by statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate profit margins and reduce consumer spending capacity. This negative demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% as of t-1mo, near historical lows suggesting vulnerability to sharp increases
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp within 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth reflects consumer spending momentum and aggregate demand strength. Sustained retail sales expansion signals robust corporate revenue growth and earnings resilience, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 726283.0M as of t-2mo indicates consumer demand baseline
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with RSAFS growth rates (Spearman rho < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production decline signals contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness. When combined with rising unemployment, this dual supply-demand shock indicates severe economic deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession fears intensify.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 2% in 3 months AND UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in same period
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.99 as of t-2mo, UNRATE at 4.3% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: financial crisis with synchronized supply-demand contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances (INDPRO down > 2% AND UNRATE up > 0.4pp), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals deteriorating real estate investment and construction sector weakness. This demand shock cascades through employment and consumer wealth channels, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1428.0K as of t-2mo, elevated from crisis lows but vulnerable
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST declines > 15% in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth reflects labor market strength and corporate hiring confidence. Sustained payroll expansion signals robust earnings growth and reduced recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices risk premium downward.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159540K as of t-1mo indicates employment level
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with monthly PAYEMS changes (Spearman rho < 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI combined with high federal funds rate signals monetary tightening regime that compresses equity valuations through dual channels: higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions are present simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL growth > 2% annualized in prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 323.364 as of t-1mo, FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances (FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND CPI growth > 2%), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth reflects aggregate demand strength and economic expansion. When GDP growth decelerates below 1% annualized rate, it signals recession risk that triggers equity repricing. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward in response to growth deceleration.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates to < 1% annualized rate in most recent quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23703.782B as of t-5mo, quarterly frequency limits real-time signal availability
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
